creator: chris.dukes.aix@gmail.com
text:
On Thu, Jun 27, 2013 at 04:54:10PM +0300, Volodymyr Kostyrko wrote:
> Hello.
> 
> Builds fine for me on stable/9 with CLANG_AS_CC. Maybe this one can
> be closed?

Yep, it still fails on build with LUA enabled and clang as the compiler.

gmake[4]: Entering directory `/wrkdirs/usr/ports/graphics/graphviz/work/graphviz-2.30.1/tclpkg/gv'
  CXX    libgv_lua_la-gv.lo
error: invalid argument '-std=gnu89' not allowed with 'C++/ObjC++'
gmake[4]: *** [libgv_lua_la-gv.lo] Error 1


Clang is being enabled with
CC=clang
CXX=clang++
CPP=clang-cpp

I'm not seeing CLANG_AS_CC in /usr/share/mk/* nor $PORTSDIR/Mk/*
Jails for builds are FreeBSD 9.1 plus patches as provided by freebsd-update
and do not seem to have this feature yet.

Specifically, I'm enabling clang as follows in the ${set}-make.conf for
poudriere

_NO_CLANG=0
.if $(.CURDIR:M*/multimedia/dirac*)
# multimedia/dirac http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=ports/173741
_NO_CLANG=1
.endif
# More of these
# You'd be surprised at how many ports are broken with clang if
# anything other than default options are used.
.if $(_NO_CLANG) == 0
CC=clang
CXX=clang++
CPP=clang-cpp
.endif


> 
> -- 
> Sphinx of black quartz, judge my vow.

-- 
Chris Dukes.  
This message is not confidential.  Due to the nature of how email is 
sent, it's only slightly more private than a postcard.  Use GPG if you 
want correspondence to be confidential and private.
